title: Signed path always reconstructed as /inbox due to missing :path header
description: In server.rs handle_inbox, the signed (request-target) path is extracted with headers.get(":path"). The :path pseudo-header is an HTTP/2 concept; it does not exist in the axum HeaderMap for HTTP/1.1 requests, which is the common case. The fallback is /inbox, so for a request to /users/{username}/inbox the verified signed string will always use path /inbox — making the path component of the signature meaningless. An activity signed for /inbox will also verify for any per-user inbox. Fix: pass the actual request path into handle_inbox (it's already available as the Path extractor in the callers) rather than looking it up in headers.
close_reason: All pass 4 fixes applied: media SSRF+size guard, mime/format consistency, proper keyId parsing, fail-closed sig verify, path reconstruction, key owner validation, media cap, blocking stdin fix, conflicts_with
